Post_Text: A benefit nobody understands is a benefit nobody values. You still paid full price for it. Open enrollment is where that waste gets locked in for a year. Here's how to stop it before your next season: [LINK]

Most charter schools run open enrollment as one binder and one rushed meeting. The schools that run it well aren't bigger or richer. They just have someone who treats it as a process worth building. Read what that process actually looks like: [LINK]

Your generalist broker dropped off a binder, sat in on one meeting, and called it service. No platform. No communication plan. No decision support. No follow-through. Dropping a binder takes an afternoon. Real enrollment takes the whole year. Here's the difference: [LINK]

One question for whoever handled your last open enrollment. How many of your staff chose a plan that fit their life, and how many just checked last year's box because nobody gave them the time or the tools? If you can't answer that, read this: [LINK]
